title: Google's Special Chip for Frozen v2: Embedding Gemini into Silicon, Token Capacity per Unit Power Increased 6 to 10 Times
excerpt: Embedding the model directly into the chip, allowing silicon to save computing power and data transfer - Google is quietly refining a new server chip codenamed
content_html:
Embedding the model directly into the chip, allowing silicon to save computing power and data transfer - Google is quietly refining a new server chip codenamed Frozen v2 along this path. According to multiple media reports, the chip aims to run the Gemini model more efficiently, with reports indicating that part of the Gemini architecture will be permanently embedded in the chip, thereby reducing the amount of computation and data movement required when answering user questions. Google engineers expect that, compared to its latest generation general-purpose AI chip, the TPU (Tensor Processing Unit), the Frozen chip can provide 6 to 10 times more token processing capability per unit of power.

The release of this chip is scheduled for 2028, but Google has clearly stated that it will not replace general-purpose TPUs, instead becoming a more specialized and targeted branch in its custom chip product line. Following the announcement, the capital market reacted quickly: Google's Class A shares (GOOGL) rose nearly 3.7% in early trading, while Class C shares (GOOG) saw a peak increase of about 3.9%.

In response to media inquiries, Google stated in a statement that the company's team continues to research and test various innovative technologies, aiming to provide users and customers with the best performance and highest efficiency. It also emphasized that although not all projects will go into mass production, this rigorous exploration is a core component of its full-stack strategy. Google further said that by co-designing hardware and software at the fundamental level, the entire system is deeply integrated and highly optimized for real-world workloads. However, Google currently positions Frozen v2 as an experimental project and has no plan to mass-produce it like the TPU.

Analysts believe that behind this project is the increasingly tight computing power situation within Google. Previously, insufficient computing power not only intensified internal resource competition, but reportedly even forced Google Cloud to reject some external customer business. Just last month, Google agreed to pay nearly $1 billion per month to SpaceX to fill the computing power gap and fulfill its computing power commitments to enterprise customers. Frozen v2 is clearly a move to alleviate this gap.

sentences.zh: 在回应媒体询问时，谷歌在一份声明中表示，公司团队持续研究和测试各种创新技术，旨在为用户和客户提供最佳性能和最高效率。

sentences.zh: 谷歌进一步表示，通过在基础层面上共同设计硬件和软件，整个系统针对现实世界的工作负载进行了深度集成和高度优化。

sentences.en: However, Google currently positions Frozen v2 as an experimental project and has no plan to mass-produce it like the TPU.
sentences.zh: 不过，谷歌目前将 Frozen v2 定位为实验项目，并没有计划像 TPU 那样进行量产。

Frozen v2 is clearly a move to alleviate this gap.

sentences.en: Analysts believe that behind this project is the increasingly tight computing power situation within Google.
sentences.zh: 分析人士认为，这一项目的背后是谷歌内部日益紧张的算力形势。

sentences.en: Previously, insufficient computing power not only intensified internal resource competition, but reportedly even forced Google Cloud to reject some external customer business.
sentences.zh: 此前，计算能力不足不仅加剧了内部资源竞争，据报道甚至迫使谷歌云拒绝了一些外部客户业务。

sentences.en: Just last month, Google agreed to pay nearly $1 billion per month to SpaceX to fill the computing power gap and fulfill its computing power commitments to enterprise customers.
sentences.zh: 就在上个月，谷歌同意每月向 SpaceX 支付近 10 亿美元，以填补算力缺口，履行其对企业客户的算力承诺。

sentences.en: Frozen v2 is clearly a move to alleviate this gap.
sentences.zh: 《Frozen v2》显然是为了缩小这一差距而采取的举措。

content_html_zh:
将模型直接嵌入到芯片中，让芯片能够节省计算能力和数据传输——谷歌正在沿着这条道路悄然完善代号为 Frozen v2 的新服务器芯片。 据多家媒体报道，该芯片旨在更高效地运行 Gemini 模型，有报道称部分 Gemini 架构将永久嵌入芯片中，从而减少回答用户问题时所需的计算量和数据移动量。 谷歌工程师预计，相比其最新一代通用AI芯片TPU（张量处理单元），Frozen芯片每单位功率可提供6至10倍的代币处理能力。

该芯片预计于 2028 年发布，但谷歌已明确表示不会取代通用 TPU，而是成为其定制芯片产品线中更专业、更有针对性的分支。 消息公布后，资本市场迅速做出反应：谷歌A类股（GOOGL）早盘上涨近3.7%，C类股（GOOG）峰值涨幅约为3.9%。

在回应媒体询问时，谷歌在一份声明中表示，公司团队持续研究和测试各种创新技术，旨在为用户和客户提供最佳性能和最高效率。 它还强调，虽然并非所有项目都会进入量产，但这种严谨的探索是其全栈战略的核心组成部分。 谷歌进一步表示，通过在基础层面上共同设计硬件和软件，整个系统针对现实世界的工作负载进行了深度集成和高度优化。 不过，谷歌目前将 Frozen v2 定位为实验项目，并没有计划像 TPU 那样进行量产。

分析人士认为，这一项目的背后是谷歌内部日益紧张的算力形势。 此前，计算能力不足不仅加剧了内部资源竞争，据报道甚至迫使谷歌云拒绝了一些外部客户业务。 就在上个月，谷歌同意每月向 SpaceX 支付近 10 亿美元，以填补算力缺口，履行其对企业客户的算力承诺。 《Frozen v2》显然是为了缩小这一差距而采取的举措。
